# Test multiple tools with coverage collection
# This demonstrates coverage being collected from different binaries

# Use mcpdiff to compare non-identical files
exec mcpdiff test1.mcp test2.mcp
stderr 'test2.mcp'

# Use mcpcat to display the content
exec mcpcat -color=never test1.mcp
stderr 'initialize'

# Create a combined trace with mcpspy
exec bash -c 'echo "test trace" | mcpspy'
stdout 'test trace'

-- test1.mcp --
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1}
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}}

-- test2.mcp --
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1}  
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}}
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","id":2}
